ogTitle: Worongary vs Wrattens Forest: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Worongary and Wrattens Forest.
heading: Worongary vs Wrattens Forest — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Worongary, Queensland and Wrattens Forest, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Worongary has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Wrattens Forest (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Worongary or Wrattens Forest safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Worongary has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Wrattens Forest scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Worongary scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Wrattens Forest scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Worongary or Wrattens Forest?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Worongary has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
